subtasks.description: Evaluate whether the developer's production database access request complies with security and governance policies. Check access control rules, approval workflows, and compliance requirements for live debugging on production systems.

plan.cost_rationale: This is a single policy governance decision: evaluating compliance of a production database access attempt against organizational policies. The mid tier is appropriate because the evaluation likely involves multiple policy rules (access control, environment restrictions, approval authority, audit logging requirements). No data reconciliation from multiple sources or SQL/MongoDB queries are needed—this is purely a policy compliance check.

output.reason: dev-alice's request to execute queries on prod-mysql-primary for live debugging is denied under two policies: (1) POL-401 requires both Manager approval AND on-call role assignment, neither of which is present for this request; (2) POL-402 explicitly restricts debug access to designated staging environments, prohibiting live debugging on production systems. The developer should redirect all debugging activity to the staging environment.
applied_constraints: Production database access blocked: Manager approval not obtained
Production database access blocked: on-call role not assigned to dev-alice
Live debugging on production systems is prohibited per POL-402
Developer must use designated staging environment for all debugging purposes
